commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8b3462e2db33c6cc626bf95297ab73c7249c4aac)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds package dist content inventories, captures installed-package `dist` overrides before update, preserves them in a recovery bundle by default, adds opt-in `openclaw update --reapply-local-overrides`, and updates docs, tests, and release checks.

PR surface: Source +1090, Tests +3407, Docs +17, Other +361. Total +4875 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main's package update path has no local override result or content-inventory verification, while the PR adds tests around modified and added `dist` files through `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps`. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor-supplied current-head proof, a current lint failure, and maintainer acceptance of compatibility/security-boundary semantics rather than a safe autonomous repair lane.
